title: Audrey Bergner En Route to Buenos Aires via Air Canada Commercial Cabin Interior Lighting
description: Buenos Aires, Argentina: Audrey Bergner sits patiently in an economy cabin seat, reflecting the quiet anticipation that precedes a significant homecoming or departure. The soft, ambient glow of the airplane’s overhead lighting washes over her, highlighting a moment of stillness amidst the rhythmic hum of long-haul travel. Her focused expression is framed by the sterile yet comforting environment of the aircraft interior, where the flicker of the seatback screen marks the transition between worlds. Beyond the oval windows, the fading daylight outside hints at the vast distances traversed to reconnect with the Argentine landscape. The composition utilizes a shallow depth of field to draw the viewer into her personal orbit, emphasizing the intimate nature of air travel as a vessel for cultural exchange. Below, shopping bags rest near her feet, serving as humble artifacts of her recent experiences and the material connection to the places she visits. This scene captures the intersection of modern convenience and the deeply human experience of movement, highlighting the logistical reality of travel photography. By documenting these transitional spaces, the image serves as a testament to the endurance required to maintain deep ties across geographical borders, reinforcing how physical movement remains central to the narrative of global migration and cultural identity. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
